name: MadridMetro_S7000
_honesty: Cloned from the Serie 9000, its near-identical AnsaldoBreda/Pininfarina sibling.
The one exterior difference modelled is the RED door band (the 9000 recoloured
that stripe blue). Figures specific to the 7000 - cab car 17.09 m, capacity 1260,
in service 2002 - are sourced below. Anything shared with the 9000, unpublished,
or contested (weight, prices, motor count, pantograph position) is marked.

motors.note: CONTESTED: vialibre-ffe.com's 7000 ficha states 'total 8 motores'. We use 16 to match the 9000; the .dat power adapts either way.

capacity_total.note: the 9000's is 1274. Different train, different figure.

payload_seated.source: the 7000's seated/standing split is not published on its own; 178 follows the family figure and the released 9000's seated-only payload convention, for collection consistency. The real total (1260) is above.

intro_year.source: http://vialibre-ffe.com/noticias.asp?not=501 - first delivery December 2001, in service 2002 (the 9000 is 2006)

length_cab_car_m.note: the 9000's cab car is 18.425 m; the 7000's is shorter. Both still round to length 8.

length_intermediate_m.source: http://vialibre-ffe.com/noticias.asp?not=501 - coches sin cabina y remolques 16.880 mm

floor_height_m.source: http://vialibre-ffe.com/noticias.asp?not=501 - altura del piso sobre carril 1.125 mm

doors_per_side_per_car.source: http://vialibre-ffe.com/noticias.asp?not=501 - 24 puertas por banda en 6 coches = 4 por coche y costado

units_built.source: http://vialibre-ffe.com/noticias.asp?not=501 - contrato de 30 trenes de seis coches
units_built.note: 37 compositions total counting the 7 bitension 7400 units.

length_carunits.formula: cab 17.09 and intermediate 16.88 m differ ~1%, far below the 1-carunit quantum, so every car rounds to the same length and each joint (L_prev - L_this)/2 is exactly zero

weight_total_t.source: a guess. AnsaldoBreda does not publish the 7000's mass and no source we found states it. Balance it before anyone plays with it.

cost.source: a guess, in cents; a balance figure set slightly below the 9000 so the older base model is the cheaper of the two

runningcost.source: a guess, per km; a balance figure

reference.note: No 7000 reference photograph in the repo yet. The livery layout (ivory with a high blue band) and the cab shape are shared with the 9000; the RED door band comes from the sources' description of the 9000-vs-7000 difference. Confirm the front colour and pantograph position against a photo before release.

_splits: AnsaldoBreda publishes no per-car breakdown of anything, so every split below
is OUR arithmetic over a sourced total, and car_totals is what forces it to
stay arithmetic: each column must add up to the fact it names, or nothing
builds. The seats are the 9000's split, unchanged - same carbody, so the two
units agree seat for seat when parked side by side. Power sits on the four
motored cars (Mc, M); the trailers (R) carry none, which is what makes them
